overview: Land an efficient threat early, then use cheap interaction to defend it and deny the opponent the time to stabilize. You are not pure aggro: you trade mana efficiently, protect your clock, and win the race on resources as well as life.
win_conditions: A protected evasive threat that ends the game over several turns
Out-tempoing the opponent until they cannot deploy meaningful blockers

keep_criteria: Two or three lands, a proactive threat, and at least one piece of interaction
A hand that does something on turn 1 or 2 and can react afterwards
Cantrips or card selection that smooth toward the right mix
mulligan_criteria: All interaction and no threat — nothing to protect
All threats and no interaction — you become fragile aggro
Land-light hands that cannot operate on curve

examples.reason: A threat, a counterspell or bounce, and lands is the textbook tempo hand.
Five reactive spells and no threat has no clock; tempo needs something to protect.

early_game: Resolve your first threat and start the clock
Hold up cheap interaction to protect it or break the opponent's curve
Use cantrips at instant speed when you would otherwise pass
mid_game: Trade interaction for the opponent's removal and key spells, not for filler
Keep attacking while leaving mana for a counter or bounce
Count whether you can afford to tap out, or must represent interaction
late_game: Avoid the long game — if resources run low, commit and force the issue
Protect the threat on the lethal turns rather than developing more

key_cards.name: Efficient threat
key_cards.notes: Usually evasive or hard to block; the rest of the deck protects it.

key_cards.name: Cheap interaction
key_cards.notes: Counters, bounce and removal that cost little, so you can act and develop in one turn.

key_cards.name: Cantrips
key_cards.notes: Smooth draws and find lands or the missing half of your hand.

sequencing_tips: Deploy threats into open mana only when you can also protect them
Cast cantrips end-of-turn when you have no better use of mana
Sequence lands so you always have the colors for your interaction

matchups.advice: Bait removal with lesser threats, then resolve and protect the real one.
Pivot interaction toward their board; your threat can also block. Stabilize, then race.

common_threats: Board wipes that undo your single protected threat
Cheap removal that out-tempos your protection
Go-wide boards that one threat cannot race
